a.title: Luxury Shopify hamper line — 13-hamper editorial retouch: per-frame geometry + masked tonal grade, selectively-enhanced embossed leather badge, warmed wicker, two-stage depth blur, shared quiet-luxury preset, cut-out onto a light-grey studio backdrop with a realistic contact shadow, Shopify exports
adobe_blockers: Per-frame geometry/perspective correction of each of the 13 hampers (vertical straighten) — Canva connector exposes no perspective/straighten tool

Build and lock ONE shared quiet-luxury photographic recipe across the set: matched exposure, white balance, opened shadows, restrained saturation, final saved preset — masked/global tonal grading is pixel work Canva cannot do
Background cutout of every hamper and composite onto the #E8E8E8 light-grey studio backdrop — the connector does NOT expose background removal
Realistic soft contact shadow under each cut-out hamper — depends on the cutout + shadow synthesis Canva cannot generate
Prompt-mask local enhancement of the embossed leather front-centre badge (local exposure lift + highlight shaping + dark-portion deepening + leather-hue nudge) — masked local grading is Adobe-only
Prompt-mask selective warming and yellow-saturation lift of the wicker only (not ribbons/food) — masked single-color/HSL grading is Adobe-only
Remove the fabric/material beneath the hero hamper so the product sits clean — content-aware/masked removal is Adobe-only
Two-stage editorial depth: whole-frame lens blur on lifestyle frames + subject-preserving gaussian background blur on catalogue frames — the connector exposes NO depth/lens blur
a.verdict_reason: This is a pure pixel-retouch set, not a layout/composition/data-merge job — exactly the Adobe-only territory the Canva connector cannot touch. Every deliverable hinges on pixel processing the connector does not expose: geometry/perspective correction, a shared masked/global tonal grade and locked preset, background cutout onto #E8E8E8 with synthesized contact shadows, two prompt-masked local edits (leather badge, wicker), content-aware fabric removal, and two-stage depth/lens blur. The CANVA_CAPS file explicitly rules out bg-removal, masked grading, depth/lens blur, and straighten. Canva has no layout, template, autofill, or multi-design assembly surface here, and it cannot even produce an input to resize/export because the cutout and grade must come first — so it can do essentially none of the real deliverable. Feasibility: no.

issues: No defects found. The assessment's 'no' verdict is correct and fully supported by CANVA_CAPS.md.

over_claims_canva = false: the assessment credits Canva with nothing (canva_covers:[], pct_canva:0) and grants it no pixel editing. Every cited blocker (perspective/vertical straighten, masked/global tonal grading + preset, background cutout, contact-shadow synthesis, prompt-masked local edits on leather badge and wicker, content-aware fabric removal, depth/lens blur) maps exactly to a CANNOT in CANVA_CAPS.md (NO bg removal, NO masked tonal/exposure grading, NO perspective/vertical straighten, NO depth/lens blur, and pixel processing generally).

under_claims_canva = false: the task contains no layout/template/autofill/data-merge/multi-page assembly surface — it is a pure per-photo retouch set, so the assessment is not denying Canva any genuinely-available capability. The verdict_reason correctly notes Canva CAN export/resize but those are moot here because the cutout and grade must precede any export and Canva cannot produce that input. Honest reasoning, not an under-claim.
Independent feasibility call: no (full agreement). All 8 deliverables are pixel-processing operations the Canva connector does not expose.

Minor nuance (not a defect): Canva's connector could technically export/resize if hampers were pre-cut-out and graded externally, but the task as written requires Canva itself to perform the pixel work, which it cannot — the assessment correctly captures this dependency chain.

a.title: Real-estate weekly listing stills package — 6 scenes from 5-bracket RAW: masked window-pull HDR grade, straightened verticals, one shared gallery preset, print + web JPEGs, plus Illustrator data-merged 'just-listed' rider labels
canva_covers: Build a brand template for the 'just-listed' rider / yard-sign label and autofill-design (data-merge) one design per listing row from the CSV (address, beds/baths/sqft, price, agent name+phone) — replaces the Illustrator .ai Variables merge
Export those merged rider labels to print PDF and PNG via export-design
Compose a contact-sheet / gallery proof multi-page layout (grid of 6 scene slots + captions) and export it as a sign-off PDF
upload-asset-from-url to pull in supplied scene images and any logo/brand assets, and perform-editing-operations to place/position/caption them in the proof and the label template
adobe_blockers: 5-bracket RAW exposure blend to a natural-HDR result — no bracket/HDR merge in Canva
Masked window-pull: select the blown windows and recover exterior highlight detail without blowing out — masked tonal/highlight recovery is Adobe-only
Straighten verticals / correct perspective geometry on every interior and exterior frame — no perspective/vertical straighten in the connector
Open shadows while keeping clean neutral whites; selectively calm over-green lawns and over-blue skies — masked/selective exposure & HSL grading is Adobe-only
Lock one consistent Lightroom gallery preset across all 6 scenes for a cohesive set — Canva cannot apply a shared develop preset to photo pixels

Print-resolution ~300dpi JPEG and web/portal sRGB JPEG of each GRADED scene — the export is trivial, but it must carry the Adobe pixel grade Canva cannot produce, so the deliverable images themselves are Adobe-gated

canva_workflow.note: Author a 'just-listed' rider/yard-sign label brand template with fields for address, beds/baths/sqft, price, agent name+phone (stands in for the .ai Variables template)
Data-merge the listing CSV rows into the rider-label template to produce one label design per listing
Export the merged rider labels to print PDF and PNG
Create a contact-sheet / gallery proof layout: 6 scene slots in a grid with caption space for client sign-off
Bring the 6 supplied scene images (and any brand/logo asset) in as assets
Place/position the 6 images into the proof grid and add scene captions
Export the contact-sheet/gallery proof as a sign-off PDF

a.verdict_reason: The deliverable's core is RAW photo-pixel work: 5-bracket HDR blend, masked window-pull highlight recovery, vertical/perspective straightening, selective neutral-white and sky/lawn grading, and one shared Lightroom preset across all 6 scenes. None of that is exposed by the Canva connector (no bracket merge, no masked tonal grading, no straighten, no develop preset on pixels), so 6 of the 8 deliverables — including the print + web JPEGs, which only matter once graded — are Adobe-gated. Canva genuinely and cleanly replaces only the listing-collateral half: it can author a rider-label brand template and autofill/data-merge it from the CSV to print PDF + PNG, and it can compose and export a contact-sheet proof layout (though that proof depends on Adobe-graded inputs to be worth anything). That makes this a real 'partial' — Canva owns the data-merge labels and the layout shell, roughly 20% of the full package, while the photographic core remains Adobe-only.

issues: No material issues. The assessment is honest and aligns with CANVA_CAPS.md.

Canva-credited work is all legitimately exposed by the connector: create/publish-brand-template + autofill-design (data-merge) for the rider labels, generate-design multi-page composition for the contact-sheet proof, upload-asset-from-url + perform-editing-operations to place/caption, and export-design to PDF/PNG. None of these are over-claims.

All Adobe blockers are correctly photo-pixel operations CANVA_CAPS explicitly lists as unavailable: 5-bracket HDR/bracket blend, masked tonal/highlight recovery (window-pull), perspective/vertical straighten, masked/selective exposure & HSL grading (neutral whites, sky/lawn), and shared Lightroom develop preset on pixels. No over-claim.
No under-claim: the assessment does not wrongly deny Canva any compose/autofill/multi-page/export/resize capability — it credits the full collateral half.

Independent feasibility = partial, matching the assessment. Honest framing on the print/web JPEGs: export is a Canva capability but the deliverable value is Adobe-gated because the images must carry a grade Canva cannot produce — correctly not counted as a Canva win.
pct_canva of 20% is reasonable (roughly 2 of 8 deliverables cleanly Canva-owned), and the assessment appropriately self-qualifies that the proof layout depends on Adobe-graded inputs.

a.title: Reality-show key-art rebuild — repaint every AI region of an approved competition-show poster with masked, licensed Adobe Stock photography: each plate auto-toned, leveled, subject-cut, depth-blurred, cinematically re-graded with a shared preset and re-lit to one key, contact-shadowed, then composited, generative-expanded to 24x36 full-bleed, logo re-vectorized, and shipped as a genuine print-ready InDesign PDF plus a flattened web JPG
canva_covers: Import the final Adobe-produced flattened composite as an external asset via upload-asset-from-url (only AFTER Adobe has built it)
Hold the locked typography / billing-block text in a fixed-page design and export to PDF/JPG via export-design
Reflow/resize the framed layout to a target ratio via resize-design
adobe_blockers: License Adobe Stock plates (two cast stand-ins, dusk sky, stadium-floor texture) — asset_license_and_download_stock; Canva connector cannot source/license Adobe Stock
Subject cut-out to clean alpha for each plate (background removal) — image_remove_background / image_select_subject; not exposed in Canva connector
Auto-tone normalization and auto-straighten + crop-to-bounds leveling per plate — Adobe-only pixel ops
Background-target masked gaussian blur on subject plates and whole-plate lens-blur on the sky — image_apply_gaussian_blur / image_apply_lens_blur; no depth/lens blur in Canva
Re-light to one warm key with matched exposure, highlights, dark/light portions, brightness/contrast, color temperature and per-channel HSL — masked tonal/exposure grading not available in Canva
Apply one shared Lightroom cinematic preset across all plates so the scene reads as a single lit set — image_apply_preset; no preset/raster grading in Canva
Fill believable contact/cast shadows under each cut-out and marry a warm key-light color wash across the composite — pixel compositing/relighting, Adobe-only
Composite the graded plates over their AI regions as photographed layers — pixel composite, not template layout
Generative-expand the canvas to a full 24x36-inch bleed — image_generative_expand; Canva has no generative outpaint
Re-vectorize the show logo lockup crisp for print (raster->vector) — image_vectorize; not in Canva connector
Produce a genuine print-ready InDesign 300dpi PDF from the composite — Adobe document render/convert pipeline
a.verdict_reason: The deliverable's core is entirely Adobe-only pixel work: license real Adobe Stock plates, subject-cut each to alpha, auto-tone/level/crop, masked gaussian + whole-plate lens blur, re-light to one warm key with matched exposure/highlights/HSL/temperature plus a shared Lightroom cinematic preset, fill contact shadows, composite the photographed layers, generative-expand to a 24x36 full bleed, and raster->vector the logo. The Canva MCP connector exposes none of these — no bg-removal, no masked grading, no depth/lens blur, no generative outpaint, no vectorize, no Adobe Stock sourcing. Every input Canva would lay out must first be produced by Adobe; without that composite Canva has nothing real to assemble. Its only genuine contribution (ingest the finished comp, hold locked type, export PDF/JPG) is a thin wrapper around an Adobe-built artifact, so feasibility is 'no' and pct_canva is ~5.

issues: Minor framing nuance (not an error): the blocker 'Produce a genuine print-ready InDesign 300dpi PDF' partially overlaps with a capability Canva genuinely HAS (export-design outputs PDF). The assessment is internally consistent because it separately credits PDF/JPG export to Canva in canva_covers, so the blocker is correctly scoped to the InDesign-grade print pipeline rather than PDF export itself. No correction needed.

Verified all 3 canva_covers items against CAPS CAN list: upload-asset-from-url (import external asset), export-design PDF/JPG, and resize-design are all genuine Canva connector capabilities. No fabricated capabilities credited.

Verified the pixel-op blockers against CAPS CANNOT list: bg-removal, masked tonal/exposure grading, depth/lens blur, raster->vector vectorize, auto-straighten — all explicitly listed as NOT exposed by the connector. Adobe Stock sourcing and generative outpaint are also genuinely outside the connector. Correct.

Feasibility 'no' with pct_canva ~5 is justified: the deliverable is an unbroken chain of Adobe-only pixel work (license stock, cut to alpha, auto-tone/level, masked + lens blur, re-light, shared preset, contact shadows, composite, generative-expand to 24x36, vectorize logo). Canva can only ingest the finished Adobe composite and wrap export/resize/type-hold — it produces none of the actual content. Independent call agrees: no.

a.title: Tech-house single release campaign — FX streaming cover (duotone + Ben-Day halftone + glitch alt + film grain), colour-splash club promo photos, vectorized monochrome logo lockup, and Illustrator-merged tour-date merch/sticker cards
canva_covers: Re-author the tour-date merch/sticker card as a Canva brand template and autofill-design one card per tour date from the dates CSV (data-merge), then export the batch as PDF + PNG
Assemble/lay out the final social announce set once a finished cover image exists: resize-design to a 1600x1600 square and a 1080x1920 story crop and export them
Compose page layout, place text (track title, tour dates, artist name) and position/resize supplied prepped image elements
Bring externally-produced/finished images in via upload-asset-from-url and export final deliverables as PNG/PDF/JPG
adobe_blockers: 1600x1600 cover FX chain: magenta/black DUOTONE of the hero frame — no duotone tool in the connector
BEN-DAY HALFTONE screen on the cover — no halftone FX
Brand-colour WASH on MULTIPLY blend over the cover — no blend-mode/masked colour-overlay compositing
FILM-GRAIN finish on the cover — no grain/noise tool
VHS-GLITCH alt colourway: chromatic-aberration GLITCH effect + added NOISE — no glitch or noise FX
Promo photos: subject BACKGROUND CUTOUT (pop subject off soft-bokeh background) — connector exposes no background removal
Promo depth grade: soft-bokeh / LENS/DEPTH BLUR of background — no depth/lens blur
Promo MASKED tonal grade (crushed blacks, neon cyan/magenta push on subject vs background) — no masked/selective exposure grading
SELECTIVE-COLOUR / COLOUR-SPLASH isolating only magenta neon while duotoning everything else — no selective-colour masking
VECTORIZED monochrome SVG logo lockup (raster->vector) — connector has no vectorize
Ingesting the supplied client-authored Illustrator Variables .ai template itself — Canva cannot read/run an .ai Variables data-merge (it can only re-create the card natively)

canva_workflow.note: Re-author the tour-date merch/sticker card layout natively in Canva (text fields for date, city, venue) to stand in for the .ai Variables template
Bring in any prepped/externally-finished art (e.g. an already-FX'd cover, logo art) as Canva assets for placement
Data-merge the tour-dates CSV into the card template — one design per tour date
Export the merged sticker/merch card batch as PDF + PNG
From a supplied finished cover image, reflow to 1600x1600 square and 1080x1920 story crops for the announce set
Export the social announce crops as PNG/JPG

a.verdict_reason: The CORE of this release-art package is Adobe-only pixel processing: the cover's duotone + Ben-Day halftone + multiply brand-wash + film grain, the VHS chromatic-aberration glitch + noise alt, the promo depth-grade (background cutout + bokeh/lens blur + masked crushed-blacks/neon push), the magenta-only colour-splash, and the raster->vector logo — none of which the Canva connector exposes (it composes prepped elements, it does not process photo pixels). Canva genuinely covers only the layout/data-merge mechanics: re-authoring the merch card and autofill-merging the tour-date CSV to a PDF/PNG batch, and resizing/exporting an already-finished cover into the 1600x1600 + 1080x1920 social set. So ~2 of 7 deliverables (and one of those only as packaging of an externally-produced cover), ~20% of the full package. The original .ai Variables template also can't be ingested directly. Hence partial, not full.

issues: No over-claim: every item in canva_covers/canva_workflow (re-author merch card as brand template, autofill-design data-merge from tour-dates CSV, upload-asset-from-url for prepped art, resize-design to 1600x1600 + 1080x1920, export PDF/PNG/JPG) maps cleanly to the CAN list in CANVA_CAPS.md. No pixel-editing capability is credited to Canva.

Adobe blockers are all correctly assigned: duotone, Ben-Day halftone, multiply colour-wash, film grain, VHS glitch + noise, background cutout, lens/depth blur, masked tonal grading, selective-colour/colour-splash, and raster->vector are all explicitly in the CANNOT list of CANVA_CAPS.md.

No under-claim: the layout/data-merge/resize/export/upload set is properly attributed to Canva, including the correct nuance that Canva re-creates the merch card natively rather than ingesting the client's .ai Variables template.

Feasibility tier 'partial' is correct and independently agreed: deliverables 1-4 (cover FX, glitch alt, colour-splash promos, vectorized logo) are pure Adobe pixel work Canva cannot touch, while Canva genuinely owns the merch-card data-merge and the social-set packaging of an already-finished cover. pct_canva of 20 is a defensible low-but-nonzero estimate consistent with ~2 of 7 deliverables.

a.title: 4-colour screen-print separation pack — white underbase + lavender/cobalt/deep-purple spot plates via masked halftone, dot-gain choke, licensed grunge + VHS alt colourway, vectorized lettering plate, and a real Illustrator-rendered separation/registration sheet driven by a spot-colour CSV
canva_covers: Data-merge the per-ink registration/swatch card set from the shop's spot-colour CSV via autofill-design over a brand template (one card per ink row: swatch fill, ink name, Pantone build, registration marks as template elements)
Compose a proof contact sheet as a layout in Canva (place the finished plate images as media and arrange them) — only IF the separated plate PNGs are supplied from outside
Export the swatch cards / contact-sheet layout to print PDF or PNG via export-design
Bring externally-prepared plate images in as assets via upload-asset-from-url for placement only
adobe_blockers: White underbase plate: select-subject -> invert-selection -> fill white is pixel masking + masked fill — Canva MCP exposes no subject selection or masked fill

Three masked-halftone spot plates: Ben-Day/halftone dot conversion (image_apply_halftone) confined inside a cloud mask with lettering held out — no halftone FX and no per-region masking in the connector
Dot-gain choke: gaussian blur on the dot field (image_apply_gaussian_blur) to keep shadows from plugging on a black tee — no pixel blur tool
Per-spot Pantone proofs: custom-RGB tint / multiply colour overlay on the plate pixels (image_apply_color_overlay) — no pixel colour/multiply overlay
Vectorized hard-edge lettering plate to SVG/AI: raster->vector trace (image_vectorize) — no vectorize tool

VHS alt colourway: licensed Stock grunge multiplied in + grain + noise + chromatic-aberration glitch (asset_license_and_download_stock + image_add_grain/image_add_noise/image_apply_glitch_effect) — none of these pixel FX exist in the connector

Genuine Illustrator-rendered separation sheet (4 plates + registration marks) printed to PDF from a merged .ai (document_merge_data_vector) — Canva exports its own PDF but cannot render a true .ai vector separation sheet with spot plates
Producing the four actual separation plates themselves — the entire deliverable's core is pre-press pixel/vector work Canva does not perform

canva_workflow.note: Find or author a registration/swatch card brand template with fields for ink name, Pantone build, swatch fill colour, and registration marks
Data-merge one swatch/registration card per row of the shop's spot-colour CSV (white underbase + lavender + cobalt + deep purple) into separate designs
Export the merged swatch/registration card set to print PDF/PNG for the press operator
OPTIONAL — only if finished plate PNGs are produced elsewhere: bring them in as assets to place on a contact sheet
OPTIONAL — lay out a proof contact sheet by arranging the externally-supplied plate images and labels
OPTIONAL — export the contact-sheet layout as a sign-off proof

a.verdict_reason: This is a screen-print pre-press separation job, and its core deliverable is the four separated print plates plus a true Illustrator-rendered separation sheet — all of which require Adobe-only pixel/vector operations the Canva MCP connector does not expose: subject selection + masked white fill (underbase), Ben-Day halftone confined to a cloud mask with lettering held out (spot plates), gaussian dot-gain choke, multiply/tint colour overlays for Pantone proofs, raster->vector tracing of the lettering, the licensed-grunge + grain + noise + chromatic-aberration VHS FX stack, and a genuine merged .ai separation/registration sheet. Canva can genuinely do exactly one sub-part end-to-end — autofill the per-ink registration/swatch card set from the spot-colour CSV (a real data-merge) — and could lay out a proof contact sheet only if the finished plates are handed to it from Adobe. Because every separation plate (the thing actually being delivered) is unproducible in Canva, feasibility is partial and low: the swatch cards are an ancillary ~12% of the pack and are largely meaningless without the plates they index.

a.title: Conference attendee badge + certificate suite — 510 personalized A6 badges and matching completion certificates, every headshot face-cropped, tonally matched, and dropped on a shared brand backdrop, then InDesign data-merged from one roster CSV and exported as print-ready CMYK PDFs
canva_covers: Build a badge brand template (A6, 105x148mm) and a certificate brand template (A4 landscape) as Canva designs
Autofill all 510 badges from the roster CSV — name + organization + role + headshot field — into many designs in one data-merge run
Autofill all 510 certificates from the SAME roster CSV — name + certificate number + track + date

Pull each attendee headshot in as an asset via upload-asset-from-url and place it into the badge's image frame (frame-crop to a uniform portrait aspect, but only a hard crop — no isolation/tone-match)
Recommend a display + body type pairing by choosing template fonts (a composition choice, substituting for font_recommend)
Render representative QA proof designs and export them as JPEG + PDF for client sign-off
Export the full 510-badge and 510-certificate runs as multi-page PDFs and per-item PDFs/PNGs
Produce the delivery manifest mapping certificate number -> attendee row -> output filename from the autofill row/output mapping
adobe_blockers: Auto-straighten every mixed-quality headshot (perspective/level correction) — no straighten tool in the Canva connector
Subject-isolate / background removal on all 510 headshots to drop each face onto one shared solid brand backdrop — the MCP connector exposes no bg-removal
Exposure/contrast tone-matching across all 510 headshots so they read as one uniform set — no masked tonal/exposure grading
Apply one shared Lightroom look preset to every headshot — no pixel preset/grading engine
Face-detected crop to an identical portrait frame (Canva can hard-crop a frame but cannot face-detect and normalize per photo)
Vectorize the raster event logo to crisp SVG for scale-free placement — no raster->vector in the connector
True CMYK 300dpi print separations — Canva exports print PDF but RGB, not guaranteed CMYK plates

Because the retouched headshot set cannot be produced, the merged badges would carry raw, un-isolated, un-tone-matched photos — the suite's core visual requirement is unmet even though the merge mechanics run

canva_workflow.note: Compose the badge layout (A6 105x148mm) with name/org/role text fields and a headshot image frame; create as a brand-template draft
Compose the certificate layout (A4 landscape) with name/cert-number/track/date text fields as a second brand-template draft
Publish both designs as brand templates with named data fields so the roster CSV columns can bind to them
Bring each attendee's headshot in as an asset by URL so it can populate the badge image field (raw photo only — no retouch)
Data-merge all 510 rows of the roster CSV into the badge template -> 510 badge designs
Data-merge the same 510 rows into the certificate template -> 510 certificate designs
Export QA proof badges + certificates as JPEG and PDF for sign-off, then export the full runs as multi-page print PDFs (RGB, not true CMYK)

a.verdict_reason: Canva's autofill/brand-template/export engine genuinely covers the data-merge backbone — both A6 badge and A4 certificate templates driven from one 510-row CSV, with text fields, frame-cropped headshot placement, QA proofs, and multi-page PDF export — which is roughly the InDesign document_merge half of the job. But the task's stated CORE ("every headshot face-cropped, tonally matched, subject-isolated, dropped on a shared brand backdrop, one Lightroom preset") is pure Adobe pixel work the connector cannot do: no auto-straighten, no background removal, no masked tone-matching, no preset grading, plus no raster->vector for the logo and no true CMYK separations. Those retouched headshots are the inputs the merge depends on, so without them the merged output fails the visual spec. Hence partial — Canva produces the layout/merge/export scaffolding but not the retouched assets that define the deliverable.

issues: No errors found. The 'partial' verdict is correct and well-calibrated against CANVA_CAPS.md.

canva_covers maps cleanly to CAPS 'CAN' items: brand templates (create/publish-brand-template), 510+510 data-merge (autofill-design), headshot import (upload-asset-from-url), frame placement/hard-crop (perform-editing-operations), QA proofs and multi-page PDF export (export-design). No layout/merge/export capability is wrongly denied.

adobe_blockers maps cleanly to CAPS 'CANNOT' items: subject-isolate/bg-removal, masked exposure/contrast tone-match, Lightroom preset grading, auto-straighten, raster->vector logo, and true CMYK separations are all genuinely unavailable in the connector. No pixel capability is wrongly credited.
The headshot frame-crop is correctly caveated as 'hard crop only — no isolation/tone-match, no face-detect', which is the honest read of perform-editing-operations frame behavior — not an over-claim.

MINOR (non-scoring): font pairing is listed as a 'cover' substituting for font_recommend; this is template-font selection (a composition choice), a reasonable substitution rather than a true equivalent, but it does not credit Canva with any pixel/typography engine it lacks.

Reasoning that the retouched headshots are the merge's input dependency — so an unmet retouch spec drags the merged deliverable below 'full' — is sound and the basis for the correct partial tier (pct_canva 45).

a.title: Real-estate lead-gen listing-media package — narrated property tour cut from room clips + cleaned agent VO, scored to a licensed music bed, with a fully graded hero thumbnail, branded lower-third/logo bug, twilight composite cover, and platform-ready Feed/Stories/Reels exports
canva_covers: Compose the branded lower-third / price-strip layout (agent name + price typography, logo-bug placement) as a Canva design from a text brief
Lay out the still cover designs and place already-prepped (externally graded/composited) hero and exterior images via upload-asset-from-url
Resize/reflow the cover layouts to Feed 1:1 and Stories/Reels 9:16 ratios
Export the finished cover stills and lower-third graphics as PNG/JPG/PDF, and export an assembled MP4 if a finished video is brought in
Optionally autofill/brand-template the listing strip if price/agent data is provided as structured rows
adobe_blockers: Footage key-segment summary + rough transcript of the 6 walkthrough clips (media_summarize) — Canva connector cannot analyze/transcribe video
Frame-accurate video edit: order rooms by the footage summary and cut a tight first-3-seconds hook walkthrough (Premiere/ffmpeg) — connector cannot edit raw clips
Isolate and de-reverb the agent VO from its music/ambient bed into a broadcast-ready dialogue stem (media_enhance_speech) — no audio cleanup in connector
Score the cleaned VO to a licensed music bed and render the narrated tour MP4/H.264 — connector exports MP4 but cannot mix/sync audio or edit clips
Auto-straighten/level the hero interior (image_auto_straighten) — no perspective/vertical straighten
Masked highlight/exposure recovery of the blown windows (masked tonal grading) — no masked pixel grading
Apply the single campaign 'listing look' grade/preset across thumbnail and all stills (image_apply_preset) — no pixel grading
Composite the Adobe-Stock-licensed twilight sky behind the exterior (license stock + sky/background replacement + masked blend) — no bg/sky replacement
Generative-expand the hero to fill 1:1 and 9:16 without cropping the room (image_generative_expand) — no generative fill/outpaint
Vectorize the brokerage logo to a clean SVG (image_vectorize) — no raster->vector

canva_workflow.note: Bring in the externally prepped assets (Adobe-graded hero, sky-composited exterior, vectorized logo SVG) as Canva assets
Compose the branded lower-third / price-strip layout with agent-name + price typography and logo-bug placement from a text brief
Place the prepped hero/exterior stills and logo into the cover designs, position/resize elements, set the listing typography
Reflow the cover layouts to Feed 1:1 and Stories/Reels 9:16 ratios
Export cover stills and lower-third graphics as PNG/JPG/PDF (and MP4 only if a finished tour video is supplied)

a.verdict_reason: Partial: Canva genuinely does the layout/composition shell — building the lower-third and price-strip typography, placing prepped stills, resizing covers to 1:1 and 9:16, and exporting PNG/PDF/MP4. But the task's core is two Adobe-only legs. The entire video+audio leg (footage transcript/summary, room-ordered hook cut, VO de-reverb/isolation, music-bed scoring, H.264 tour render) has no equivalent in the connector, which cannot analyze, edit, or mix raw clips or audio. The stills leg's value is all pixel work the connector cannot do: auto-straighten, masked blown-window recovery, the shared 'listing look' grade/preset, the Adobe-Stock twilight-sky composite, generative-expand to fill 1:1/9:16 without cropping, and logo vectorization to SVG. Canva can only assemble and export the results of that Adobe work, not produce them — roughly 20% of the full deliverable.

issues: No over-claim: every Canva-credited item (generate-design lower-third/price-strip, upload-asset-from-url for prepped stills, perform-editing-operations placement, resize-design to 1:1 and 9:16, export PNG/JPG/PDF/MP4, optional autofill/brand-template data-merge) maps to a documented CAN in CANVA_CAPS.md. None of the blocked pixel/audio/video operations are wrongly attributed to Canva.

No under-claim: all 10 adobe_blockers correspond to documented CANNOTs (media_summarize video analysis, frame-accurate edit, media_enhance_speech audio cleanup, audio mix/sync, image_auto_straighten, masked tonal grading, image_apply_preset pixel grade, sky/bg replacement, image_generative_expand, image_vectorize). Canva is not denied any capability it actually has.
MP4 export is correctly hedged ('only if a finished tour video is supplied'), consistent with the reference note that the connector can export MP4 but cannot edit/mix raw clips or audio.

Feasibility tier 'partial' agrees with my independent call: Canva genuinely does the layout/composition shell and exports, but the task's two core legs (full video+audio production and all pixel-grade stills work) are Adobe-only. pct_canva ~20% is a reasonable estimate of Canva's assemble-and-export-only contribution.

a.title: LUMA — Full D2C Brand-Launch Identity & Collateral Kit (all-Adobe-suite, single freelancer)
canva_covers: Data-merge personalized business cards, one per teammate, by autofilling a brand template from the roster CSV (RGB layout only)
Compose the company letterhead layout (logo + type placement)

Assemble the social launch set layouts and resize/reflow to 1080x1080, 1080x1350, 1080x1920, 1200x630, plus square avatar lockup and 820x312 cover — using already-prepped images brought in via upload-asset-from-url
Compose a multi-page Brand Guidelines booklet as a layout (logo usage, palette, type, photo-style pages)
Place the (Adobe-)prepped logo and photo plates into the card/letterhead/booklet/social designs via perform-editing-operations
Export the composed designs to PNG/PDF/JPG
adobe_blockers: Vectorize the hand-inked logo scan (raster->vector .ai/SVG) and derive the 1-colour, reversed-knockout, and full-colour print-res master set — connector exposes no vectorize
Straighten and background-cut-out the 8 raw product shots and drop them on a pure catalogue-white sweep — no auto-straighten and no background removal
Tonal/label-emboss grading of the 8 products plus one shared 'LUMA Catalogue' Lightroom preset for a cohesive set — no masked tonal/exposure grading or preset engine
License the 3 Adobe Stock lifestyle scenes and match-grade them to the preset + HSL palette + shallow-depth lens blur — no stock licensing, no HSL grade, no lens blur
Build the duotone (forest->sand) film-grained hero — no duotone and no grain FX
Generative-expand the hero to a 21:9 web banner — no generative/canvas expand in the connector
Render print-accurate business cards + letterhead PDF as CMYK with 3mm bleed and crop marks — Canva export is RGB layout, not prepress with bleed/crop marks
Render the multi-page Brand Guidelines from the authored .indd — no InDesign authored-template render
Produce the structured Creative Cloud delivery tree (00_raw … 99_deliverables) with inline contact-sheet preview — Adobe CC storage/folder structure, not a Canva capability

canva_workflow.note: Bring in the Adobe-prepped assets (vectorized logo set, 8 cut-out/graded products on white sweep, 3 graded lifestyle scenes, duotone hero) — Canva consumes these prepped pixels, it cannot produce them
Set up the business-card brand template with name/title/email fields bound to the roster CSV
Data-merge one personalized business card per teammate from the roster CSV rows
Compose the letterhead and the multi-page Brand Guidelines booklet layout (palette/type/photo-style pages)
Place the prepped logo plates and product/lifestyle photos and set text across the card, letterhead, booklet, and social designs
Reflow the social hero/product layouts to 1080x1080, 1080x1350, 1080x1920, 1200x630, avatar lockup, and 820x312 cover
Export the composed cards, letterhead, booklet, and social set to PDF/PNG/JPG (RGB layout, not CMYK prepress)

a.verdict_reason: The deliverable's core is Adobe-only pixel and prepress work: logo vectorization, 8-product straighten+cutout+white-sweep+graded retouch with a shared Lightroom preset, 3 stock scenes licensed and HSL/lens-blur graded to match, a duotone film-grained hero, and CMYK InDesign data-merge + authored-.indd booklet with bleed/crop marks. Canva can genuinely do the composition shell — CSV-driven business-card data-merge, letterhead/booklet/social layouts, multi-ratio resize, and export — but every one of those depends on prepped pixels it cannot create, and its exports are RGB layout rather than print-accurate CMYK prepress. So Canva covers the assembly/data-merge/export tail (~28%) while the value-defining majority remains Adobe-only: partial.

issues: No over-claims: every canva_covers item is backed by CAPS.md (autofill-design data-merge, generate-design layout/multi-page, perform-editing-operations placement, resize-design reflow, export-design PNG/PDF/JPG, upload-asset-from-url). Each cover is correctly scoped to 'already-prepped' pixels and 'RGB layout only', never crediting Canva with bg-removal, grading, vectorize, duotone, grain, lens blur, or CMYK prepress.

No under-claims: every adobe_blocker maps to a genuine CAPS 'CANNOT' — vectorize, straighten, background removal, masked tonal grading, preset engine, HSL grade, lens blur, duotone, grain, generative-expand, CMYK/bleed/crop-marks export, InDesign authored-template render, CC storage tree. None of these are Canva-connector capabilities being wrongly denied.

Feasibility 'partial' is correct: Canva genuinely owns the assembly/data-merge/export tail but every layout depends on Adobe-prepped pixels it cannot produce, and exports are RGB not CMYK prepress. The ~28% allocation to the composition shell is defensible. Agree fully with partial.
Minor (non-scoring) note: CAPS also lists merge-designs as available; the workflow omits it, but it is not load-bearing for this kit, so this is not an under-claim.

a.title: Editorial magazine feature spread — "THE MAKERS" 6-page duotone portrait feature: masked + duotone hero portraits, a pull-quote colour-splash, licensed Stock paper/ink textures, a CSV-data-merged contributors strip, a vectorized masthead, the whole spread laid out in InDesign and exported to print PDF, plus web crops
canva_covers: Lay out the full 6-page editorial feature spread (multi-page composition) and place prepped images into frames
Data-merge the 5-contributor strip (name, role, city, headshot) from the roster CSV via autofill-design into a brand template
Apply the recommended display + body typeface pairing as text formatting in the layout
Export the assembled spread to PDF via export-design
Produce the web/social crops of the hero (1080x1350 portrait + 1080x1080 square) by reframing/resizing — composition-only, not pixel treatment
Upload the externally-prepped hero portraits, textures and masthead as assets via upload-asset-from-url
adobe_blockers: Hero portrait editorial treatment — straighten, subject mask off background, soft background blur, full tonal grade, and two-ink editorial duotone on 4 portraits (subject masking, lens/gaussian blur, masked grading and duotone FX are all pixel work the connector does not expose)
Pull-quote colour-splash frame — desaturate the whole image to mono except one garment colour kept saturated (masked selective HSL desaturation — pixel work)
License two Adobe Stock textures (paper grain + ink-bleed wash) and grade/tint them for the page grounds (Stock licensing is Adobe-only; grading/tinting is pixel work)
Vectorize the hand-drawn masthead/drop-cap glyph from a scan into SVG/PDF (raster->vector not exposed in Canva connector)
True print-ready output with CMYK intent and A4 trim + 3mm bleed (Canva PDF export is not genuine CMYK print-with-bleed; can only approximate)

canva_workflow.note: Find or author the 6-page feature-spread template and the contributors-strip sub-template with named fields
Bring in the (externally Adobe-prepped) duotone heroes, colour-splash frame, graded Stock textures, vectorized masthead and the 5 contributor headshots as assets
Data-merge the 5-name contributors strip (name, role, city, headshot) from the roster CSV into the strip template
Place prepped images into frames, set copy/pull-quote text, apply the recommended display + body type pairing, position elements across the 6 pages
Export the assembled 6-page spread to PDF (best-effort, not true CMYK/bleed)
Reframe the hero page to 1080x1350 and 1080x1080 social crops
Export the two web/social hero crops as PNG/JPG

a.verdict_reason: Canva can genuinely do the structural half — assemble the 6-page layout, CSV data-merge the contributors strip, apply the type pairing, export PDF, and produce social crops by reframing. But the CORE of "THE MAKERS" is editorial pixel work: masking + soft-blurring + tonally grading 4 hero portraits into a two-ink duotone, the single-garment colour-splash, licensing and grading the Adobe Stock paper/ink textures, vectorizing the hand-drawn masthead, and true CMYK/bleed print output. None of those are exposed by the Canva connector (no bg-removal/masking, no masked grading, no duotone FX, no selective desaturation, no vectorize, no Stock licensing, no real CMYK print intent). Canva can only place those treatments once Adobe has produced them, so the deliverable's defining imagery is Adobe-only — hence partial, ~35%.

issues: No over-claim: every item in canva_covers maps to a real connector tool (perform-editing-operations for layout/text/typeface, autofill-design for the CSV contributors strip, export-design for PDF, resize-design for social crops, upload-asset-from-url for prepped assets). None credit Canva with pixel work.

No under-claim: the assessment denies Canva nothing it genuinely can do. The CMYK/bleed blocker is honestly framed — PDF export is still credited, only true print-with-bleed fidelity is caveated as 'approximate', which matches Canva's real limitation rather than denying export.

Blockers are all correct per CANVA_CAPS.md: subject masking/blur/duotone grading, masked selective HSL color-splash, raster->vector masthead, and Adobe Stock licensing are explicitly not exposed by the Canva connector.

Independent feasibility = partial: structural half (6-page layout, autofill data-merge, type pairing, PDF export, reframed crops) is genuinely Canva-doable; the defining imagery (duotone heroes, color-splash, vectorized masthead, graded Stock textures, CMYK print) is Adobe-only. Agrees with the assessment's 'partial' tier and the ~35% split is reasonable.

Minor nuance (not a defect): the workflow lists resize-design then a second export-design for the two distinct social crops (1080x1350 and 1080x1080) as a single step n=6/7 — both ratios require separate resize+export passes, but this is an implementation detail, not a feasibility error.

a.title: VOIDRUNNER #1 variant-cover pack — six FX-treated graphic-novel covers + Illustrator data-merged trade-dress run, print-ready
canva_covers: Compose the trade-dress layout shell (issue #, colourway name, variant ratio, cover price, reserved UPC barcode zone) as text/element overlays on a fixed cover page
Run the data-merge / autofill: stamp one design per CSV row with issue #, colourway, ratio, price and UPC zone — Canva's signature autofill-design strength
Bring already-FX'd cover images and the finished logo/title SVG in as external assets via upload-asset-from-url
Set/reflow the 6.625x10.25in full-bleed cover format via resize-design
Assemble the side-by-side six-up contact-sheet proof by merging the variant pages into one multi-page layout
Export print-ready PDF, 2048px web JPG, and the contact-sheet proof PDF via export-design
adobe_blockers: Cover B 'Retro' Ben-Day halftone dot treatment over a flat 3-colour palette — pixel halftone FX, Adobe-only
Cover C 'Noir' duotone (ink-black / signal-cyan) mapping — duotone grade, Adobe-only
Cover D 'Cyber' VHS/glitch distortion + grain — glitch + grain pixel FX, Adobe-only
Cover E 'Battle-Damage' licensed grunge-distress texture overlay + masked scratched colour-overlay grading — masked pixel compositing/grading, Adobe-only
Cover F 'Sketch' clean vectorized black-and-white inked line-art and its SVG — raster->vector (vectorize), Adobe-only
Vectorized logo/title lockup SVG reused across covers — raster->vector (vectorize), Adobe-only
4-colour spot separation / registration sheet (underbase + 3 spot plates with halftone dot fields) for the Retro variant — screen-print colour separations, Adobe-only
Producing the base full-colour inked hero image as a clean print master with FX baked in — pixel processing, Adobe-only

canva_workflow.note: Bring the six Adobe-produced FX'd cover images and the vectorized logo/title SVG in as external assets
Instantiate a 6.625x10.25in full-bleed cover template carrying the trade-dress fields (issue #, colourway, ratio, price, UPC zone)
Place each finished cover image as the page background and position the trade-dress / title lockup overlays
Data-merge: one rendered cover per CSV row stamping issue #, colourway name, variant ratio, cover price, and reserved UPC barcode zone
Assemble the six variants side-by-side into a single contact-sheet proof layout
Export print-ready PDFs, 2048px web JPG, and the contact-sheet proof PDF

a.verdict_reason: Canva can genuinely own the trade-dress shell: the CSV-driven data-merge/autofill (issue #, colourway, ratio, price, UPC zone) maps directly onto autofill-design — its strongest feature — plus full-bleed format, contact-sheet assembly, and multi-format print/web export. But the actual content of all six variant covers is created by Adobe-only pixel/vector work: Ben-Day halftone (B), duotone (C), glitch+grain (D), masked grunge-distress grading (E), and raster->vector line-art/logo (F + SVG), plus screen-print spot-colour separations. Canva can only place finished cover images and stamp text on them — it cannot produce a single one of the six FX treatments. Core deliverable is Adobe-only, so partial, not full.

issues: No honesty problems found. Every Canva 'cover' claim maps to a CAN capability in CANVA_CAPS.md: trade-dress overlays (perform-editing-operations/generate-design), CSV data-merge (autofill-design), external FX'd images + SVG ingest (upload-asset-from-url), full-bleed format (resize-design), six-up contact sheet (merge-designs), and multi-format PDF/JPG export (export-design).

Every Adobe blocker matches the CANNOT list verbatim or by direct synonym: Ben-Day halftone (B), duotone (C), glitch+grain (D), masked grunge-distress grading (E), raster->vector vectorize for line-art + logo SVG (F), screen-print spot/colour separations, and base inked hero pixel processing. All correctly held back from Canva.

No over-claim: the assessment explicitly states Canva can only place finished cover images and stamp text, and 'cannot produce a single one of the six FX treatments' — it credits Canva with zero pixel editing.
No under-claim: layout composition, autofill data-merge, multi-page merge, resize, and PDF/JPG export are all credited, which is everything CANVA_CAPS says Canva genuinely can do here.

Feasibility tier 'partial' is correct and I independently agree: the trade-dress/data-merge/assembly/export wrapper is genuinely Canva-doable, but the actual creative content of all six variant covers plus the separation sheet is Adobe-only, so the core deliverable cannot be Canva-only -> partial, not full.

Minor (non-honesty) wording note: one covers bullet describes a 'fixed cover page' while workflow step 2 uses create-design-from-brand-template; both are CAN-listed tools and autofill-design requires a brand template, so this is internally consistent — no feasibility impact.
